name: Alzira to La Casella Trail
name_original: Paraje Natural La Casella - Trail run por el Pas del Pobre
description: This difficult 9.9-mile (15.9 km) jogging trail in La Casella Natural Park offers challenging ascents and panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alzira offer diverse terrain, from river valleys to mountain peaks, shaped by the Sierra de Corbera mountain range and the Júcar River basin. The region features the protected Paraje Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella, nestled between the Sierras del Cavall Bernat and Les Agulles. This natural park provides varied landscapes, including karstic formations and lush flora. The area's extensive trail networks are suitable for various outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Alzira The most popular jogging route is Cavall Bernat Panoramic Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic vistas over the surrounding natural parks. Another top favourite among local runners is Alzira to La Casella Trail, a difficult 9.9 miles (15.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads towards viewpoints like L'Ouet and Mirador de La Casella. Local runners also love the La Murta Icehouse – Creu del Cardenal loop from Paratge Natural Municipal la Murta, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through the Paraje Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Alzira is defined by the Sierra de Corbera, the Júcar River basin, and the protected La Murta y la Casella natural park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to routes requiring good fitness. The routes in Alzira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Alzira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alzira?
A: Alzira offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 50 trails available for various skill levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain peaks, ensuring a varied experience for every runner.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Alzira?
A: The running routes in Alzira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning panoramic views, and the well-maintained paths within areas like the Paraje Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Alzira?
A: Yes, Alzira has several routes suitable for beginners. While many trails offer significant elevation, there are at least 2 easy routes available. These paths provide a gentler introduction to jogging in the region, often found in the river valleys or less strenuous sections of the natural parks.

Q: What are some challenging running trails for experienced runners in Alzira?
A: Experienced runners looking for a challenge will find plenty of options, with over 20 difficult routes available. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832215867/cavall-bernat-panoramic-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cavall Bernat Panoramic Trail&lt;/a&gt; (5.8 miles / 9.4 km with over 400m elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832264821/alzira-to-la-casella-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alzira to La Casella Trail&lt;/a&gt; (9.9 miles / 15.9 km with nearly 500m elevation gain) offer significant climbs and rewarding views.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while jogging in Alzira?
A: Jogging in Alzira allows you to experience a rich variety of natural features. You'll encounter the Sierra de Corbera mountain range, the Júcar River basin, and the protected Paraje Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella. This natural park is known for its diverse flora, dramatic karstic formations, and stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4552325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Ouet&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas over the surrounding landscapes and the Levantine coast.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the running paths?
A: Absolutely. Many running paths in Alzira pass by significant historical and cultural sites. A notable example is the ruins of the 14th-century fortified Jerónimos monastery, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5608443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Valldigna&lt;/a&gt;, nestled within the La Murta y La Casella Municipal Natural Area. You might also encounter old lime kilns, the Fuente de la Murta, and aqueducts, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Icehouse – Creu del Cardenal loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available near the popular running areas in Alzira?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the main access points for popular running areas, particularly around the Paraje Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information, especially during peak times, as spaces can fill up.

Q: Can I reach jogging trails in Alzira using public transport?
A: While some routes might be accessible via local bus services to the outskirts of Alzira, many of the more scenic and challenging trails within the natural parks are best reached by car. For specific public transport options, it's recommended to consult local transport schedules depending on your starting point.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Alzira?
A: For families, the natural parks offer some accessible paths, though many routes have elevation changes. Look for trails with less steep inclines and well-maintained surfaces. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4462728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides an engaging experience with natural beauty and historical elements that can be enjoyed by families.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Alzira?
A: Alzira benefits from a mild Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for jogging almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, the climate is generally mild, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alzira?
A: Yes, Alzira features several excellent circular running routes. Many trails within the Paraje Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella are designed as loops, allowing runners to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Icehouse – Creu del Cardenal loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular example, offering a diverse landscape and historical sights.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Alzira?
A: Dogs are generally permitted on many of the outdoor trails in Alzira, especially within the natural parks like La Murta y la Casella. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash and ensure you follow any specific park regulations regarding pets to protect the local wildlife and environment.
tips: Water fountain with rest area and very dense and cool trees for hot days. It is reached by the La Simeta path or by the PR-CV 303 forest track from the La Casella car park.

Panoramic point with very nice views of the Valley of La Casella and the city of Alzira. It is reached by a path in good condition and without technical difficulty. From here you can continue ascending to the viewpoint of l'Ouet.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Alzira to La Casella Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. Near Alzira, you might find more gradual, paved sections. However, as you venture into the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella, expect earth tracks, stony paths, and even stone stairs. Some parts involve narrow and stony paths with continuous ascents, especially on more challenging sections like the Pas del Pobre. The landscape features dense vegetation, short palm trees, and impressive carstic formations.

faqs.question: How difficult is this trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of difficult grade for jogging. While initial sections from Alzira might be more gradual, many paths within the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella involve significant elevation changes and challenging terrain. Routes like the PR-CV 304 to La Ratlla have high difficulty due to substantial elevation gain, and a run through the Pas del Pobre is explicitly labeled as a 'Hard run' requiring very good fitness and sturdy shoes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you can enjoy stunning natural beauty and panoramic views. Notable highlights include the Font del Garrofer and the La Casella Viewpoint, which offers expansive vistas. From higher points like La Ratlla, you can see the surrounding mountains, neighboring regions, and even the Mediterranean Sea. The area also features a deer reserve and various fountains.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through sections of several other trails. You'll encounter parts of the Des d'Alzira a la Casella, Ruta dels Monestirs - El Pas del Pobre, Pic de la Ratlla, Pista a la Font del Garrofer, and the Senda de la Font de la Teula.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella enjoys a favorable microclimate with mild temperatures. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, offering pleasant weather for jogging. Summers can be hot, so if you go then, it's best to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat. Winters are mild, making it a good option year-round, though some higher sections might be cooler.

faqs.question: Is the Alzira to La Casella Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the diverse wildlife, including a deer reserve, and to ensure the safety and enjoyment of all visitors. Be prepared to carry out any waste.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required to access the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella for general recreational activities like jogging. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the park, especially concerning protected areas or specific activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: For accessing the trail from Alzira, you can often find parking options within the town. To get closer to the La Casella Natural Park itself, there are parking areas available, such as near the Forest House of La Casella or the Bar La Casella-Font del Garrofer. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, you might find facilities near the start in Alzira or at specific points within the park, such as near the Bar La Casella-Font del Garrofer. The area also features several natural fountains, but it's always wise to carry sufficient water, especially on longer or more challenging sections, as their reliability can vary.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy trail running shoes with good grip. Hydration is crucial, so carry plenty of water. Depending on the season, sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is recommended. A small first-aid kit, a fully charged phone, and navigation tools (like the komoot app) are also advisable, especially for less familiar sections.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The specific Komoot Tour 'Alzira to La Casella Trail' is designed as an out-and-back route. However, within the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella, there are many intersecting paths and trails, allowing for the creation of various loop options if you wish to explore further and customize your run.
